plan_metadata.name: Feature: UI Layout & Create Project Questions

steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option within the "New Project" dropdown menu to create an agent using instructions and actions.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" at coordinates (474, 210).
Click on the dropdown option "Tab" under the "Teams Capability" menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or specify the project directory.

Click the "Explorer" icon in the Visual Studio Code sidebar to open the file navigation panel. The click is targeted at the top-left vertical toolbar, directly on the first icon, ensuring access to project files and folders.

Reduce the zoom level of the Visual Studio Code interface using the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + -; this affects all UI elements, including the sidebar (showing Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit) and the main editor area.
@assertion Verify that there are 6 sections on left panel: accounts, environment, development, lifecycle, utility, help and feedback.
@assertion Verify that lifecycle section has provision, deploy and publish commands.
@assertion Verify that utility section has "Zip App Package", "Validate Application", "Publish to Store in Developer Portal" commands.

Click the "Create New Agent/App" option in the DEVELOPMENT section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar in Visual Studio Code, where the tooltip "Create a new agent/app from scratch or begin with a sample app." is visible.
@assertion Verify that there are 5 options “Declarative Agent”, "Custom Engine Agent", "Copilot Connectors", "Teams Agents and Apps" and "Office Add-in"
Click the "Teams Agents and Apps - Apps for Microsoft 365" option in the "New Project" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion  verify there are 4 options: "General Teams Agent", "Teams Agent with Data", "Teams Collaborator Agent" and "Other Teams Capabilities".

Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" setup dialog in Visual Studio Code, highlighting the selection for creating a Teams agent that chats with users using the Teams AI Library and LLMs.
@assertion  verify there are some options like: "Customize", "Azure AI Search" and "Custom API".
Click the "Customize" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent with Data" setup dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu of the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" dialog within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there are  options like this: Tab, Message Extension and Simple Bot"
Click the "Back (Ctrl+Alt+←)" button in the Teams Capability quick selection popup at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Back" button (top-left, marked by a red crosshair) in the Teams Agent or App creation dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion verify options like : no action, add an action, add a copilot connector and start with typespec for microsoft 365 copilot
Click the "Add an Action" option in the "Create Declarative Agent" dropdown menu within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair.

@assertion verify options like below:
 start with a new api
create an action with a new api from azure functions, 
start with an openapi description document
create an action form your existing api, 
start with an office add-in action
create a declarative agent with office add in action, 
start with an MCP server
create actions from your existing mcp server.
Click the highlighted "Start with a New API" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface, under the "Create New Agent/App" workflow.
@assertion verify there's 4 options: none, api key, microsoft entra and oauth.
Click the drop-down field labeled "Select an authentication type" in the "Authentication Type" dialog within Visual Studio Code, at the top-center of the interface.
Click the "Back (Ctrl+Alt+←)" button in the "Create an Action" modal at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface to return to the previous selection screen.
Click the text input field at the top of the "Create Declarative Agent" dropdown in Visual Studio Code, to start entering or selecting an action for the agent creation workflow.
Click on the "Custom Engine Agent" option in the "New Project" popup dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion verify there's two options: basic custom engine agent and weather agent.
